_comment: v4rian pool/* creation restriction. Pins pool/* ref creation to the allocator workflow's identity (the repo's github-actions[bot] using the default GITHUB_TOKEN). Developer pushes of pool/* refs from laptops are rejected at the server. Bypass via the workflow run is the only allowed path; the workflow validates the actor and then creates both pool/[vYYYY.MAIN/]nN.* and safety/[vYYYY.MAIN/]nN.* atomically. The include patterns cover both layouts: legacy flat refs/heads/pool/<suffix> and current refs/heads/pool/<vYYYY.MAIN>/<suffix> (the major-version prefix that mirrors the vault and archived-safety schemes). Apply via .io.v4rian.how-git/scripts/apply-repo-rules.sh, or POST directly to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets after substituting the {ACTOR_ID} placeholder with the integration id of the GitHub Actions bot on the target repo (gh api /orgs/{org}/installations --jq '.installations[] | select(.app_slug=="github-actions") | .id').
name: v4rian-pool-creations
